title: Equivalent fractions
description: Practice making equivalent fractions by relating their denominators.

hints:
Let's represent $\dfrac{1}{3}$ using a visual model.

![rectangle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d7cb4dd833f8e80ce08a7b0e58380717ea59a2d3)

How many sixths will we need to shade to have the same amount shaded as $\dfrac{1}{3}$?

![rectangle with 1of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 6 equal parts, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aaae56285ae349c2776683550ed69ff1aab8842d)

We need to shade $\greenD2$ sixths.

![rectangle with 1of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f69e5742ecd7118bf2fa8fd408449575d4fb200)

 $\dfrac{1}{3} = \dfrac{\greenD2}{6}$
$\greenD{t}=\greenD{2}$

Let's represent $\dfrac{1}{2}$ using a visual model.

![rectangle with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 half](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/46d5290e176c7614b397ecda983c0eaacc001d78)

How many eighths will we need to shade to have the same amount shaded as $\dfrac{1}{2}$?

![rectangle with 1of 2 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 half. same sized rectangle below it, with 8 equal parts, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eab22ef7a1edd06a3b2aa316642ea117ce701c94)

We need to shade $\greenD4$ eighths.

![rectangle with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 half. same sized rectangle below it, with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31dd66b88d20c30aaadc4448f7ebd9728131e0f1)

 $\dfrac{\greenD4}{8} = \dfrac{1}{2}$
$\greenD{m}=\greenD{4}$

Let's represent $\dfrac{6}{8}$ using a visual model.

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd23df65b1e4af77c8942458c19802d7cce786be)

How many fourths will we need to shade to have the same amount shaded as $\dfrac{6}{8}$?

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth. same sized rectangle below it, with 4 equal parts, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7e80871c564b814eb7b78351eef77bc62e3cd8b8)

We need to shade $\greenD3$ fourths.

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5df0da1b0d9bf292e9f082ac0357c8b9304834d3)

 $\dfrac{\greenD3}{4} = \dfrac{6}{8}$
$\greenD{p}=\greenD{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What value of $k$ makes the equation true?**

 $\dfrac{4}{k} = \dfrac{2}{3}$

$k=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Equivalent fractions cover the same area of the whole.

Let's represent $\dfrac{2}{3}$ using a visual model.

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f4c867d5e2196c4febd679e4b81ff39e677aeeb4)

We need $4$ parts in the equivalent fraction to cover the same area as $\dfrac{2}{\blueD{3}}$.

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f4c867d5e2196c4febd679e4b81ff39e677aeeb4)  
![same-sized rectangle with 4 of an unknown number of equal parts shaded, each labeled 1 over unknown number.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abf8c5b8eb780336f389488ffd3fed76c6538020)

How many equal parts should the equivalent fraction have?

We need $\maroonD{6}$ equal parts per whole.

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f4c867d5e2196c4febd679e4b81ff39e677aeeb4)  
![same-sized rectangle with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3455bd7cf49f4f99e66f7ea1e1ecc2a92ec44621)

 $\dfrac{4}{\maroonD{6}} = \dfrac{2}{3}$
$\maroonD{k}=\maroonD{6}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What value of $w$ makes the equation true?**

 $\dfrac{1}{4} = \dfrac{2}{w}$

$w=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Equivalent fractions cover the same area of the whole.

Let's represent $\dfrac{1}{4}$ using a visual model.

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7ddf8c939d7e89178d1e60687e729b8c982e151)

We need $2$ parts in the equivalent fraction to cover the same area as $\dfrac{1}{\blueD{4}}$.

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7ddf8c939d7e89178d1e60687e729b8c982e151)  
![same-sized rectangle with 2 of an unknown number of equal parts shaded, each labeled 1 over unknown number.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e959a9cca19c8808f5b3a520a073b9a58821888)

How many equal parts should the equivalent fraction have?

We need $\maroonD{8}$ equal parts per whole.

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7ddf8c939d7e89178d1e60687e729b8c982e151)  
![same-sized rectangle with 2 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36ca9771d8eede22007715f1e2af81d0a0ba97b8)

$\dfrac{1}{4} = \dfrac{2}{\maroonD{8}}$
$\maroonD{w}=\maroonD{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What value of $c$ makes the equation true?**

 $\dfrac{3}{6} = \dfrac{4}{c}$

$c=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Equivalent fractions cover the same area of the whole.

Let's represent $\dfrac{3}{6}$ using a visual model.

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cdea38c22eaf93cee9be36e4cef6a4edeaf17d80)

We need $4$ parts in the equivalent fraction to cover the same area as $\dfrac{3}{\blueD{6}}$.

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cdea38c22eaf93cee9be36e4cef6a4edeaf17d80)
![same-sized rectangle with 4 of an unknown number of equal parts shaded, each labeled 1 over unknown number.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d6b7e0533c80adc575dc9ed52b57c0a931dcb52c)

How many equal parts should the equivalent fraction have?

We need $\maroonD{8}$ equal parts per whole.

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cdea38c22eaf93cee9be36e4cef6a4edeaf17d80) 
![same-sized rectangle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/470468218d6701200fd8166e407053f307f62445)

$\dfrac{3}{6} = \dfrac{4}{\maroonD{8}}$
$\maroonD{c}=\maroonD{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following fractions are equivalent to $\dfrac24$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For fractions to be equivalent, they must represent the same area of the whole.
Let's use a visual model to see which fractions shade the same area of the whole as $\blueD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$.

![rectangle with 2 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 half](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c9b301115246e979221febdca75032a7b44522a5)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{4}} = \greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/de25b3d6e8b8141e161448f996bbfb78be9a548a)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{4}} \neq \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{6}}$

![rectangle with 2 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ac6f3a25f883bb266efcf1f30671229a7427a1d)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{4}} = \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$

![rectangle with 2 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/71afbb5d6d4b8a1268b0843dda71e84ec13788b3)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{4}} \neq \goldE{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

The following fractions are equivalent to $\blueD{\dfrac24}$:

- $\greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$
- $\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following fractions are equivalent to $\dfrac36$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For fractions to be equivalent, they must represent the same area of the whole.
Let's use a visual model to see which fractions shade the same area of the whole as $\blueD{\dfrac{3}{6}}$.

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth. same sized rectangle below it, with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 half](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/363b1a864ee84b42a63a49ce556f56ce7aa13c5e)

$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{6}} = \greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth. same sized rectangle below it, with 5 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/408fbd85d0a4d93963240af400367f766c30de8e)

$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{6}} \neq \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth. same sized rectangle below it, with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3777fab223436475913576b13639e00696b4488a)

$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{6}} = \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$

![rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 sixth. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/555c2a8ff29a198da586823c2ff7e8bae104e722)

$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{6}} \neq \goldE{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

The following fractions are equivalent to $\blueD{\dfrac36}$:

- $\greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$
- $\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following fractions are equivalent to $\dfrac13$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For fractions to be equivalent, they must represent the same area of the whole.
Let's use a visual model to see which fractions shade the same area of the whole as $\blueD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$.

![rectangle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/06d30561e7af631ebdede28123a20e87ffcc5a15)

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{3}} \neq \greenD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$

![rectangle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7b966a973cf852de08addfdc50498afccd98063e)

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{3}} \neq \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{6}}$

![rectangle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fb4f6697e24ee02b8e56611a80e06914bf038567)

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{3}} = \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{6}}$

The following fraction is equivalent to $\blueD{\dfrac13}$:

- $\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{6}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following fractions are equivalent to $\dfrac6{8}$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For fractions to be equivalent, they must represent the same area of the whole.
Let's use a visual model to see which fractions shade the same area of the whole as $\blueD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$.

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth. same sized rectangle below it, with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 third](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/893d84de650466a3dccc5de75e5593a0ecc97f42)

$\blueD{\dfrac{6}{8}} \neq \greenD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45c9e24d8d7016a831f2f7c039f3049e0b06ff00)

$\blueD{\dfrac{6}{8}} \neq \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{6}}$

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 eighth. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5877be1fc4f4e416e20e338b08d7d1ad35c63c23)

$\blueD{\dfrac{6}{8}} = \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

The following fraction is equivalent to $\blueD{\dfrac68}$:

- $\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following fractions are equivalent to $\dfrac14$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For fractions to be equivalent, they must represent the same area of the whole.
Let's use a visual model to see which fractions shade the same area of the whole as $\blueD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$.

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce9129276c3b04d50ab812d6252f7e61d3266c71)

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{4}} \neq \greenD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 half](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f683e81f4c3ef963025d099aaf07b04bdbf07d01)

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{4}} \neq \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$

![rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 fourth. same sized rectangle below it, with 2 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a05cf15f1c809c841c21b5fdb458432e0c18ce87)

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{4}} = \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$

The following fraction is equivalent to $\blueD{\dfrac14}$:

- $\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following fractions are equivalent to $\dfrac23$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For fractions to be equivalent, they must represent the same area of the whole.
Let's use a visual model to see which fractions shade the same area of the whole as $\blueD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$.

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 4 of 6 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 sixth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45cd2b1ea272856ed0e037912fe0c6c3a2fffa7e)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{3}} = \greenD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 7 of 8 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 eighth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/964c7d26760f2264af1b581ef5af14fd2f2ffadb)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{3}} \neq \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, with each part labeled 1 third. same sized rectangle below it, with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, each labeled with 1 fourth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4e51bde570550be410ff9bd98ae9f62df9d1a45e)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{3}} \neq \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

The following fraction is equivalent to $\blueD{\dfrac23}$:

- $\greenD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$
